tweets.title: On this day in history, 16 years ago, Satoshi Nakamoto explained that mining more and more Bitcoin transactions “does NOT slow down your generation rate. When generate is scanning hashes, it only hashes the header of the block, which is a constant size.”
As of last night, new bitcoins are 10.09% easier to mine as the network programmatically adjusted difficulty at block 953,568, marking the 11th-largest automatic decreased change in history.

tweets.title: On this day in history, 16 years ago, a developer created a website giving away 5 bitcoin for free to anyone, anywhere. The ‘faucet’ only required a user to complete CAPTCHA, verifying you were not a bot, and distributed 19,700 bitcoin in total, worth more than $1 billion today.
On this day in history, 17 years ago, the word “cryptocurrency” was posted for the first time.
On opening day of the previous football World Cup, the price of one bitcoin was only $16,712.
On this day in history, 13 years ago, a baby was born through the world’s first documented fertility treatment ever paid for using bitcoin.
